title: Doppelgängers

jokes.text: women and minorities can vote, we have indoor toilets, and we no longer burn widows for learning arithmetic
jokes.setup: Explaining historical differences between 1817 and now
jokes.punchline: Escalating from voting rights to burning widows for arithmetic
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation structure with absurd historical detail that feels period-appropriate while being darkly funny

dialogue.speaker: April Ludgate
dialogue.line: In 1817, only white men could vote. Now anyone can vote. Progress!

dialogue.speaker: April Ludgate
dialogue.line: In 1817, if your wife did your taxes wrong, you could burn her. Now you can't. So really, we've gone backwards.

jokes.text: It is horse-dancing, madam.
jokes.setup: Speaker dismissively calls dressage 'a fancy horse-riding thing'
jokes.punchline: Overly serious correction to 'horse-dancing'
jokes.explanation: Perfect economy and character specificity - pompous Eagletonian defending their fancy hobby

dialogue.speaker: Ben Wyatt
dialogue.line: It's not horse-riding, it's horse-dancing.

dialogue.speaker: Leslie Knope
dialogue.line: Horse-dancing?

dialogue.speaker: Ben Wyatt
dialogue.line: Yes. Horse-dancing.

jokes.text: All you horse dancing people, sit in your saddles if you will.
jokes.punchline: Treating them literally like they're on horses in the meeting
jokes.explanation: Quick callback that extends the absurdity naturally

dialogue.speaker: Ron Swanson
dialogue.line: I would watch that.

dialogue.speaker: April Ludgate
dialogue.line: It's called dressage.

dialogue.speaker: Ron Swanson
dialogue.line: No, I mean I would watch the meeting if everyone was treating it like they're on horses.

jokes.text: Not surprised, I'm one of a kind, just like the custom Nikes I designed that say 'Tommy's Tootsies' across the toes.
jokes.setup: Tom claims he's one of a kind
jokes.punchline: Comparing himself to ridiculous custom shoes
jokes.explanation: Pure Tom Haverford - his narcissism and terrible fashion sense perfectly combined

dialogue.speaker: Tom
dialogue.line: I'm one of a kind. I'm like those custom shoes they make in Italy.

dialogue.speaker: Tom
dialogue.line: Except instead of being handcrafted by artisans, I was mass-produced in a factory, and instead of being worth thousands of dollars, I'm worth about forty bucks.

jokes.text: - Hey, everybody. - No! No, why? This can't be happening. Why, Leslie?
jokes.punchline: April's immediate horror and despair
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment - April's over-the-top reaction to Jerry is exactly right

dialogue.speaker: Jerry
dialogue.line: Good morning, everyone! It's a beautiful day!

dialogue.speaker: April
dialogue.line: Oh God, no. Why would you say that? You've doomed us all.

jokes.text: Welcome back, Jerry Gergic.
jokes.punchline: Yet another wrong version of his name
jokes.explanation: Standard running gag execution - not particularly creative but functional

dialogue.speaker: Leslie
dialogue.line: Jerry, I have your new business cards.

dialogue.speaker: Jerry
dialogue.line: Oh, great! Thanks, Leslie.

dialogue.speaker: Leslie
dialogue.line: They say 'Gerry Gergich, Parks Department.'

dialogue.speaker: Jerry
dialogue.line: It's Jerry. Jerry Gergich.

dialogue.speaker: Leslie
dialogue.line: Right, right. Well, the printer said they're non-refundable.

jokes.text: It's Jerry. No, it's Gary. Your name is Larry, Larry Gengurch. His name is Larry Gengurch.
jokes.setup: Jerry tries to clarify his real name
jokes.punchline: Everyone confidently stating different wrong names
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of the running gag with multiple characters piling on

dialogue.speaker: Jerry
dialogue.line: My name is actually Garry. G-A-R-R-Y.

dialogue.speaker: April
dialogue.line: Oh, so it's Larry.

dialogue.speaker: Tom
dialogue.line: No, he said Gary. G-A-R-Y.

dialogue.speaker: Jerry
dialogue.line: No, Garry. With two Rs.

dialogue.speaker: April
dialogue.line: Right, Larry.

jokes.text: Come on, April, it's already hard enough for my family when you call me 'Gary.'
jokes.punchline: Revealing it affects his family life
jokes.explanation: Adds emotional weight to the running gag while maintaining the cringe

dialogue.speaker: Jerry
dialogue.line: My wife doesn't even call me Jerry anymore. She just sighs and points at things.

dialogue.speaker: Leslie
dialogue.line: That's... that's actually kind of sad, Jerry.

dialogue.speaker: Jerry
dialogue.line: My name's not even Jerry.

jokes.text: Ron is basically a better version of George Washington.
jokes.punchline: Comparing Ron to America's founding father
jokes.explanation: Perfect Leslie hyperbole that actually makes sense given Ron's character

dialogue.speaker: Leslie
dialogue.line: Ron, you're like America's founding father, except instead of fighting the British, you're fighting the government. Which is ironic because you work for the government.

jokes.text: Like a majestic alley-oop. You're all amazing! You're all fired. Teamwork.
jokes.setup: Ben and Chris describing their teamwork system
jokes.punchline: The jarring contrast between motivation and firing people
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character comedy showing their complementary personalities with perfect economy

dialogue.speaker: Ben
dialogue.line: We have a system. I motivate people with positive reinforcement and careful planning.

dialogue.speaker: Chris
dialogue.line: And I fire people.

dialogue.speaker: Ben
dialogue.line: It's a good system.

jokes.text: I said, 'Gayle, put that bikini away, because Pawnee needs me.'
jokes.setup: Jerry explaining why he came back from retirement
jokes.punchline: The mental image of Gayle in a bikini and Jerry's heroic delusion
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Jerry's misplaced self-importance and unfortunate imagery

dialogue.speaker: Jerry
dialogue.line: I came back because I realized I missed working here. Plus, Gayle in a bikini... let's just say it motivated me to get back in shape.

dialogue.speaker: Jerry
dialogue.line: I'm going to be a hero. A real hero. Like a fireman, but better.

jokes.text: Oh ho! Somebody forgot a banana in here--ooh!
jokes.setup: Jerry cheerfully noting something in his desk
jokes.punchline: Implied physical comedy of him getting hurt by the rotten banana
jokes.explanation: Standard Jerry physical comedy - predictable but in character

dialogue.speaker: Jerry
dialogue.line: Oh, a banana! I'll just keep this in my desk for a healthy snack later.

jokes.text: Oh, that is the perfect name for you. I love it. Never change it! Wasn't gonna.
jokes.setup: Craig's over-the-top enthusiasm about Donna's name
jokes.punchline: Donna's matter-of-fact response
jokes.explanation: Perfect character contrast - Craig's intensity vs Donna's cool confidence

dialogue.speaker: Craig
dialogue.line: Donna! What a beautiful name! It's so strong, so powerful! Donna could be a warrior queen, or a successful businesswoman, or a dolphin trainer!

dialogue.speaker: Donna
dialogue.line: It's just a name, Craig.

jokes.text: I want to be a Spanish man named Terrence, but that didn't happen.
jokes.setup: Craig complaining about his boring name
jokes.punchline: His bizarrely specific alternative identity wish
jokes.explanation: Perfectly random and specific character detail that reveals Craig's dramatic personality

dialogue.speaker: Craig
dialogue.line: My name is Craig. It's the most boring name in the world. You know what I wish my name was? Thunderbottom. Thunderbottom Silk Mane.

jokes.text: I carried the Eagleton department on my shoulders for years, and I loved every second of it. You don't even know!
jokes.punchline: His emotional intensity about office management
jokes.explanation: Good establishment of Craig's over-the-top personality and work obsession

dialogue.speaker: Craig
dialogue.line: I don't need a vacation. I need to be here, managing this office. Every day I come in and I give one hundred and ten percent. You know what that means? It means I'm overextended and probably gonna have a heart attack by fifty-two.

jokes.text: No, it's Tynnyffer with two y's. I used to be Jennifer, but then I decided to rebrand myself.
jokes.punchline: Ridiculous spelling and treating name change as 'rebranding'
jokes.explanation: Good character establishment showing Eagleton pretentiousness and marketing mindset

dialogue.speaker: Tynnyfer
dialogue.line: Hi, I'm Tynnyfer.

dialogue.speaker: Leslie Knope
dialogue.line: That's... an interesting spelling.

dialogue.speaker: Tynnyfer
dialogue.line: I know. I rebranded myself. I used to be Jennifer, but I added a 'Y' and two 'N's to make it more unique.

jokes.text: Oh, wait, hang on. It's Xanax o'clock.
jokes.punchline: Casual drug dependency treated as routine
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about prescription drug culture with great economy

dialogue.speaker: Tynnyfer
dialogue.line: Yeah, I take pills to wake up, pills to sleep, pills to stay focused during the day. My doctor says I'm doing great.

jokes.text: My name's April, and I just wanted to say that your dress is so cute it's bonks.
jokes.punchline: Using ridiculous slang 'bonks' while faking niceness
jokes.explanation: Perfect April character moment - her sarcastic mimicry of valley girl speak

dialogue.speaker: April
dialogue.line: Oh my gosh, you guys! This is so exciting! I am literally thrilled about everything right now! This project is going to be absolutely bonking amazing!

dialogue.speaker: April
dialogue.line: And I just want to say, you all are doing such a bonking great job. Really, truly bonking fantastic work, everyone.

jokes.text: She's the worst person I've ever met. I want to travel the world with her.
jokes.punchline: Contradictory statements revealing her perverse fascination
jokes.explanation: Brilliant April moment showing her love of awful people and perfect deadpan delivery

dialogue.speaker: April
dialogue.line: Tynnyfer is the worst. She's stupid, she's annoying, and she has no redeeming qualities whatsoever. I hate everything about her. Also, she has amazing hair and I think she's really cool and funny. But seriously, I can't stand her.

jokes.text: Is that your name, or are you telling me you're finished talking? Both. Dunne and done.
jokes.setup: Ron asking about the name Dunne
jokes.punchline: Pun on done/Dunne with minimal explanation
jokes.explanation: Solid wordplay with excellent economy and character-appropriate delivery

dialogue.speaker: Ron Swanson
dialogue.line: Dunne? Like the name?

dialogue.speaker: Other Character
dialogue.line: Yes, why?

dialogue.speaker: Ron Swanson
dialogue.line: Sounds like you're done.

jokes.text: I guess that explains the shoulder pads.
jokes.punchline: Mocking his supposedly outdated fashion
jokes.explanation: Decent burn but not particularly original or character-specific

dialogue.speaker: Tom
dialogue.line: I don't understand how these new phones work.

dialogue.speaker: Ragiv
dialogue.line: Tom, that phone came out three years ago.

dialogue.speaker: Tom
dialogue.line: Exactly. Ancient history.

dialogue.speaker: Ragiv
dialogue.line: You're wearing a Bluetooth headset from 2006.

dialogue.speaker: Tom
dialogue.line: This is vintage, Ragiv. Vintage is in.

jokes.text: How did he notice these?
jokes.punchline: Tom's horror that his fashion choice is noticeable
jokes.explanation: Good character beat showing Tom's vanity and fashion insecurity

dialogue.speaker: Tom
dialogue.line: Wait, wait, wait. You can see the shoulder pads? I thought they were subtle.

dialogue.speaker: Tom
dialogue.line: That's the whole point of shoulder pads — nobody's supposed to notice them!

jokes.text: Pawnee has the very first documented case of mega-diabetes. And the only know occurrence of Lou Gehrig's other disease.
jokes.punchline: Ridiculous made-up diseases
jokes.explanation: Creative absurdist humor about Pawnee's unhealthiness with good medical parody

dialogue.speaker: Ann
dialogue.line: Pawnee has the highest rate of spontaneous baldness in the entire country.

dialogue.speaker: Ron
dialogue.line: That doesn't sound right.

dialogue.speaker: Ann
dialogue.line: We also have an epidemic of what we call 'Pawnee Fever.' Symptoms include fever, confusion, and the inability to tell the difference between a hot dog and a calzone.

dialogue.speaker: Jerry
dialogue.line: I have that!

dialogue.speaker: Ann
dialogue.line: Yes, you do, Jerry.

jokes.text: With your tiny little doll feet.
jokes.punchline: Specific odd detail about her feet
jokes.explanation: Classic Leslie weird-specific compliment that feels genuine despite being odd

dialogue.speaker: Leslie
dialogue.line: Ann, you are such a good friend, and you have really nice feet. Like, disproportionately nice feet. They're like little baby feet, but on an adult.

jokes.text: Oh, my God, look at that. It's waffles! - Delicious waffles.
jokes.setup: Ann trying to have serious conversation
jokes.punchline: Leslie's waffle obsession derailing the moment
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Leslie's avoidance through her signature obsession

dialogue.speaker: Ann
dialogue.line: Leslie, we need to talk about something serious.

dialogue.speaker: Leslie
dialogue.line: Oh my god, yes. I've been thinking about waffles all morning. Do you think they're better with whipped cream or without? Because I'm leaning towards with, but I could see an argument for without.

dialogue.speaker: Ann
dialogue.line: Leslie...

dialogue.speaker: Leslie
dialogue.line: Wait, what if you did half and half? Like, whipped cream on one side, nothing on the other? Ann, this could change everything.

jokes.text: Joe Biden on a horse shirtless. That's amazing!
jokes.punchline: Completely random distraction that only Leslie would find amazing
jokes.explanation: Perfect Leslie absurdity - her specific political enthusiasm and avoidance tactic

dialogue.speaker: Leslie
dialogue.line: Wait, did you know that penguins have knees? They do! Look it up! They're just hidden under their feathers. Isn't that incredible? I've been thinking about penguin knees all morning and I completely forgot what we were talking about.

jokes.text: who wants to stay in the greatest town in the world with her best friend and be happy forever when she can abandon her soul sister like an old shoe and move to a garbage city full of jerks
jokes.setup: Leslie claiming she understands Ann's decision
jokes.punchline: Increasingly bitter and specific descriptions
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing Leslie's passive-aggressive hurt through hyperbole

dialogue.speaker: Leslie
dialogue.line: I totally understand. You need to do what's best for you. I get it. I support you 100%. Even though you're abandoning me. Abandoning us. After everything we've been through. After I made you my best friend. After I carved out a special place in my heart just for you. A place I can never get back. A place that will now be filled with nothing but regret and a sort of low-level resentment that will probably affect all my future relationships.

jokes.text: We've just been like blah blah blah blah blah blah blah! Like, talking, like, so much forever.
jokes.setup: April pretending to bond with Tynnyfer
jokes.punchline: Obviously fake enthusiasm with literal 'blah blah blah'
jokes.explanation: Perfect April sarcasm disguised as valley girl enthusiasm

dialogue.speaker: April
dialogue.line: Oh my god, that's so great, Tynnyfer. Blah blah blah, that sounds amazing. Blah blah blah.

jokes.text: This is, like, the best day ever. I know, I'm eating it all up.
jokes.punchline: April's deadpan delivery of valley girl speak
jokes.explanation: Continues the character comedy of April's perfect mimicry

dialogue.speaker: April
dialogue.line: Like, oh my god, I'm like, so totally into this bonding thing. Like, literally, this is like, the best day ever. I'm like, so blessed right now. Like, I can't even.

jokes.text: instead of wiling away like a dying turd flower
jokes.punchline: Gross and oddly specific metaphor
jokes.explanation: Weird Leslie metaphor that's gross but memorable

dialogue.speaker: Leslie
dialogue.line: Friendship is like a three-legged dog — it's messy, it doesn't make sense, and someone's always getting dragged somewhere they don't want to go.

jokes.text: Ew, no. I've had so much rejuvenation that I don't think a baby could get out of there if it tried.
jokes.punchline: Disturbing oversharing about plastic surgery
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about cosmetic surgery culture with good character specificity

dialogue.speaker: Tynnyfer
dialogue.line: I have two kids. Well, I have two kids now. I used to have three, but I had them surgically removed and combined into two larger, more efficient children.

jokes.text: Oh, I have a medical condition all right. It's called caring too much! And it's incurable! Also I have eczema.
jokes.punchline: Dramatic declaration followed by mundane addition
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation with perfect Craig over-dramatics and mundane deflation

jokes.text: I'm a yoga nut. And I'm a nut nut. They make delicious milks, man.
jokes.punchline: Pun on 'nut' with hippie enthusiasm
jokes.explanation: Simple pun that establishes the anti-Ron character effectively

dialogue.speaker: Ron Swanson
dialogue.line: I'm very fond of nuts. Macadamia nuts, Brazil nuts, walnuts... I love all kinds of nuts.

jokes.text: You only eat vegetables that have been thrown out in people's dumpsters.
jokes.punchline: Disgusting specificity of dumpster diving
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist escalation of hippie lifestyle extremes

dialogue.speaker: Ron Swanson
dialogue.line: A freegan is someone who eats only food that would otherwise be discarded. They dumpster dive for their meals.

dialogue.speaker: Leslie Knope
dialogue.line: It's actually a very sustainable lifestyle.

dialogue.speaker: Ron Swanson
dialogue.line: It's disgusting. I once saw a freegan pull a half-eaten rotisserie chicken out of a dumpster behind a grocery store. He inspected it, shrugged, and took a bite.

dialogue.speaker: Leslie Knope
dialogue.line: That's... that's not typical.

dialogue.speaker: Ron Swanson
dialogue.line: He then pulled out a binder labeled 'Chicken Rankings by Dumpster' and made notes.

jokes.text: My trusty sandals. I believe a man's feet should remain uncaged. Same goes for all chickens.
jokes.punchline: Equating human feet with animal rights
jokes.explanation: Good character comedy showing the hippie Ron's philosophical consistency

dialogue.speaker: Ron Swanson
dialogue.line: I wear these boots because they're practical. A man needs proper footwear for the work he does. These boots have lasted me fifteen years.

dialogue.speaker: Ron Swanson
dialogue.line: Now, I know what you're thinking — 'Ron, aren't those made of leather?' Yes. Yes they are. And I have no problem with that. A cow gave its life so I could have reliable footwear. That's the natural order.

dialogue.speaker: Ron Swanson
dialogue.line: What I won't do is wear shoes made from some endangered species. That's not about animal rights — that's about respecting the balance of nature. If you wear a shoe made from a creature that only has six left in the world, you're a fool and a communist.

jokes.text: I don't want to put this on your plate. You have a lot to deal with.
jokes.punchline: His fake consideration while manipulating
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Tom's manipulative 'helpfulness'

dialogue.speaker: Tom
dialogue.line: Oh, I'll handle Eric. Don't worry about it.

dialogue.speaker: Tom
dialogue.line: I'm thinking... I'm really considering all the angles here... Okay, here's what we're gonna do.

jokes.text: I've got the condo in Seattle, the fiance in Denver. - Huh? - It won't last.
jokes.punchline: Casual dismissal of her relationship
jokes.explanation: Perfect Donna moment - casual about relationships with great deadpan delivery

dialogue.speaker: Donna
dialogue.line: I could leave him. I got options.

jokes.text: He had, and I quote, 'no comment.'
jokes.setup: Leslie complaining about Ben's reaction to fashion
jokes.punchline: Formal quoting of his boring response
jokes.explanation: Good Leslie character moment showing her dramatic interpretation of normal responses

dialogue.speaker: Leslie
dialogue.line: Ben saw my new outfit and just said, 'That looks nice.' That looks nice? That's it?

dialogue.speaker: Ben
dialogue.line: What do you want me to say?

dialogue.speaker: Leslie
dialogue.line: I don't know, something with more energy! 'Leslie, you look absolutely stunning in that outfit. The way that color brings out your eyes is simply mesmerizing.' But no, I get 'That looks nice.'

jokes.text: I'm just trying to stop time with legally-binding friendship contracts.
jokes.punchline: Leslie's matter-of-fact explanation of her insane plan
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Leslie absurdity - perfectly captures her control issues and government mindset

dialogue.speaker: Ben
dialogue.line: Leslie, what is this? You've signed contracts with every vendor in the city.

dialogue.speaker: Leslie
dialogue.line: Yes. I have. Because I'm going to need them all for the Harvest Festival.

dialogue.speaker: Ben
dialogue.line: The Harvest Festival is in three weeks.

dialogue.speaker: Leslie
dialogue.line: I know. That's why I've already signed the contracts. If I waited until two weeks before, I'd be behind schedule.

jokes.text: I made unity cookies with the Pawnee town's original colors of blue and yellow. However, I did not have yellow frosting, so I had to use mustard.
jokes.setup: Leslie trying to make peace with cookies
jokes.punchline: Using condiments as frosting
jokes.explanation: Good Leslie character moment showing her dedication despite poor execution

dialogue.speaker: Leslie
dialogue.line: I'm going to make the most beautiful cookies with frosting made from... ketchup.

dialogue.speaker: Ben
dialogue.line: Leslie, no.

dialogue.speaker: Leslie
dialogue.line: And mayonnaise swirls!

dialogue.speaker: Ben
dialogue.line: Leslie, please stop.

dialogue.speaker: Leslie
dialogue.line: A little mustard accent for tartness.

jokes.text: Mmm! Sugar mustard.
jokes.setup: Leslie trying her own terrible cookies
jokes.punchline: Her positive reaction to disgusting combination
jokes.explanation: Good physical comedy showing Leslie's eternal optimism

dialogue.speaker: Leslie
dialogue.line: Mmm, these are... these are really something. The chocolate chips and the anchovies really complement each other.

jokes.text: You're not 'Ann' pretty, but you have potential.
jokes.punchline: Backhanded compliment using Ann as the beauty standard
jokes.explanation: Perfect Leslie character moment showing her Ann-centric worldview

dialogue.speaker: Leslie
dialogue.line: You look great today, Evelyn. I mean, you're almost as pretty as Ann.

jokes.text: I'm halfway through designing a bamboo gazebo as a tribute to the founders of motown.
jokes.punchline: Bizarre project connecting random elements
jokes.explanation: Great absurdist character moment showing Craig's artistic pretensions

dialogue.speaker: Craig
dialogue.line: I'm busy. I'm working on a project that connects all the dots between seemingly unrelated things.

dialogue.speaker: Craig
dialogue.line: Like, did you know that if you take the number of letters in 'Parks and Recreation,' multiply it by the average temperature in Pawnee, and divide it by the number of times I've said 'treat yo self,' you get the exact coordinates of where I buried my time capsule?

jokes.text: Best friends, attack Ron!
jokes.setup: Leslie's manic attempt to control the situation
jokes.punchline: Commanding her 'friends' like an army
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of Leslie's desperation and control issues

dialogue.speaker: Leslie
dialogue.line: Okay everyone, listen up! You're going to be on my team whether you like it or not. Ron, you're in charge of woodworking and scowling. April, I need you to be sarcastic and unhelpful. Tom, just... exist near me and occasionally say something about yourself. Ben, you're my deputy, which means you do exactly what I say and look concerned about budgets. And Donna? Just be Donna. Everyone clear? Great! We're going to make this work if it kills me! Which it won't, because I have a seventeen-step contingency plan!

jokes.text: You are so amazing right now. You're like skinny Mother Theresa.
jokes.punchline: Bizarre comparison adding 'skinny' to Mother Teresa
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Tynnyfer's shallow worldview and weird compliments

dialogue.speaker: Tynnyfer
dialogue.line: April, you're like a skinny Mother Teresa.

jokes.text: It's Dwayne Wade's house. I got his address off the Internet. I really hope he's there when she walks in and he throws a basketball at her head.
jokes.setup: April's talking head about her prank
jokes.punchline: Escalating to violent fantasy
jokes.explanation: Perfect April dark humor with great escalation and character consistency

dialogue.speaker: April
dialogue.line: I decided to prank Jerry by putting a 'kick me' sign on his back. But then I thought, why stop there? I could put a 'punch me' sign. Then a 'stab me' sign. Then I could actually stab him. Not fatally, just like, in the shoulder or something. Then I could go to jail, break out of jail, and stab him again.

jokes.text: Those who cross the czar feel the wrath of the czar!
jokes.punchline: Over-the-top autocratic declaration
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of Leslie's control issues with historical reference

dialogue.speaker: Leslie
dialogue.line: I have decided to declare myself Parks Director for life. All decisions will be made by me, and me alone. Anyone who disagrees will be reassigned to the gravel pit. Also, I'm making it illegal to eat lunch at your desk. Punishable by exile.

jokes.text: geography will never change your feelings
jokes.punchline: Ron's practical wisdom delivered matter-of-factly
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ron moment - wise advice delivered with minimal emotion

dialogue.speaker: Ron
dialogue.line: Leslie, I'm going to give you some advice. You need to treat your friendship with Ann like a government budget. You're spending money you don't have, and it's only going to end badly. Cut her loose.

jokes.text: I once thought I had a friend. Then it turned out he was the single worst person I have ever met.
jokes.punchline: His typically dark take on relationships
jokes.explanation: Great Ron character moment with perfect deadpan delivery of pessimistic wisdom

dialogue.speaker: Ron
dialogue.line: Friendship is two people using each other to avoid loneliness. When it stops being mutually beneficial, one or both of you will leave. It's not personal; it's just human nature. I have three best friends and I trust none of them.

jokes.text: Giving in to hate is like drinking saltwater. The thirst only grows worse.
jokes.punchline: Pretentious wisdom that's actually reasonable
jokes.explanation: Good character contrast showing Other Ron's earnest philosophy vs. Real Ron's cynicism

dialogue.speaker: Ron Swanson
dialogue.line: I believe that all government is a waste of taxpayer money. Except for national defense, and maybe... roads.

dialogue.speaker: Leslie Knope
dialogue.line: Ron, that's actually... kind of reasonable?

dialogue.speaker: Ron Swanson
dialogue.line: Don't sound so surprised. I have principles.

jokes.text: After I defeated him in an epic struggle, E.R.I.C. is gone.
jokes.setup: Tom explaining what happened with the computer
jokes.punchline: Treating tech replacement as heroic battle
jokes.explanation: Good Tom character moment showing his ego and tendency to dramatize

dialogue.speaker: Tom
dialogue.line: So I went to IT, right? And I told them my computer was broken. They said they'd replace it, but it would take two weeks. Two weeks! So I said, 'You know what? I'm going to fight for this. I'm going to march back to that office every single day until you give me a new computer.' And you know what happened? They gave me a new computer the next day.

dialogue.speaker: Tom
dialogue.line: I'm basically a war hero now.

jokes.text: my DVR is 13 months pregnant with episodes of Scandal
jokes.punchline: Describing his DVR backlog as pregnancy
jokes.explanation: Brilliant metaphor that's both original and perfectly captures modern TV watching habits

dialogue.speaker: Craig
dialogue.line: My DVR is like a pregnancy. It started out as something I was excited about, but now I'm just terrified and I don't know how it's going to end.

jokes.text: we were always just beat at the end of the day. I actually think it's something else.
jokes.setup: Ben reflecting on their accounting partnership
jokes.punchline: Realizing their sadness wasn't from work but loneliness
jokes.explanation: Good character development moment with emotional insight

dialogue.speaker: Ben
dialogue.line: You know, Chris, I was thinking about our time in the accounting department together.

dialogue.speaker: Chris
dialogue.line: Yeah, those were some dark days.

dialogue.speaker: Ben
dialogue.line: The darkest. I remember thinking it was because of the spreadsheets, the budget cuts, the endless reports.

dialogue.speaker: Chris
dialogue.line: Right, the work was soul-crushing.

dialogue.speaker: Ben
dialogue.line: But I just realized something. It wasn't the work that made us sad.

dialogue.speaker: Chris
dialogue.line: What do you mean?

dialogue.speaker: Ben
dialogue.line: We were lonely. We were just two guys in a room full of numbers, with no one to share it with.

dialogue.speaker: Chris
dialogue.line: Oh man. That's... actually really sad.

jokes.text: A cherry tomato. Nope. A gumball.
jokes.punchline: Finding what looks healthy but isn't
jokes.explanation: Simple visual gag that reinforces the Pawnee health theme

dialogue.speaker: Chris
dialogue.line: I'm looking for something healthy to eat. Do you have any quinoa bowls?

dialogue.speaker: Jerry
dialogue.line: We have a kale salad.

dialogue.speaker: Chris
dialogue.line: Perfect. What's in it?

dialogue.speaker: Jerry
dialogue.line: Kale, bacon, ranch dressing, and deep fried croutons.

dialogue.speaker: Chris
dialogue.line: How much bacon?

dialogue.speaker: Jerry
dialogue.line: A lot.

dialogue.speaker: Chris
dialogue.line: I'll have the burger.

tmdb_overview: Leslie tries to calm a minor scandal that gets blown out of proportion. Tom tries to impress a new love interest, while Ben convinces Ron to prepare a will.
insight: Six doppelgängers identified across 54 jokes—absurdist escalation anchors a high-density character showcase.
